meta.originalTitle: Sea Change for Salinity 土地盐碱化

children.text: One of the most serious problems facing Australian farmers is an increase in the salt content in the soil. However, there are new weapons emerging in the fight against salinity.

Beneath the flat, impassive surface of Australia lie hidden mountains, valleys and gorges – ancient traps and channels for the deadly salt that is stealthily killing so much of the Australian landscape. The war on salt is calling forth new weapons. A suite of high technologies used by geologists to see underground and prospect for gold and minerals is now being used to pinpoint the presence of salt beneath the landscape, and to predict where it might move.

Unless this process is clearly understood, warns Chief of Exploration and Mining Dr Neil Phillips, the hard work now underway of planning and tree-planting on the surface may be rendered ineffective: salt can still sneak past and erupt, following one of the ancient river channels formed millions of years ago. The use of airborne electromagnetics to detect salt hidden beneath the landscape has been around for a decade, but the past two years have seen a major development in its precision and powers of detection. Like the use of radar in battles, it has the potential to turn the tide of the struggle in favour of the defence by helping to pinpoint, plot and predict the movements of the foe.

Angus Howell, who farms near Warrenbayne, in Southeast Australia, saw his first outbreak of salt in 1948. Over the ensuing decades the patches spread and multiplied until they consumed almost 100 hectares. By the late 1970s, Howell and his fellow farmers had decided it was time for action and established a government-funded “Landcare” group in a bid to save Australia's farmland. But despite a mounting effort by scientists, farmers and governments, the “white death” continued to encroach. Small successes were eclipsed by larger defeats and fresh outbreaks.

"The technical solutions just aren't there yet for dealing with broadacre salinity, nor are the social and economic solutions. How do you introduce the land-use changes that are needed when people still need to make a living?” Howell asks. There is no satisfactory solution yet. Part of the problem has lain in salt's ability to mount ambushes, emerging somewhere new, sometimes unexpected and unexplained, beating plans to intercept it. Only now are scientists starting to really disclose its secret subterranean stores and passages.

The need for such knowledge is pressing. Salt has already afflicted six million hectares of once-productive country. At present rates, it is predicted that, by 2050, it will have sterilised a total of 17 million hectares and the waters of Australia's Murray River will regularly exceed the World Health Organisation's salt limits for drinking water. Defeating this assault may take centuries, not decades.

Electromagnetic surveys measure the electrical conductivity of soil to reveal the distribution of salt and the nature and variability of the regolith – the weathered rock and sediment that may lie above the bedrock. Magnetic surveys measure small differences in the Earth's magnetic field, enabling scientists to probe the deep past and reconstruct ancient landscapes – rivers, basins and faults now buried under tens of metres of sediment. These features help to reveal where groundwater is stored, dictate the direction of groundwater movement, and are critical to predicting or ruling out salinity hot-spots.

Radiometric analysis is based on the detection of radiation emitted by elements contained in rocks and soils, allowing scientists to delineate landforms. These factors influence the mobility of salt through the soil profile and help determine where to plant particular crop species to tackle the problem. Using data from the Murray River region, scientists have revealed a network of ancient drainage systems that channelled water beneath the current land-surface. These buried channels may carry salt and sometimes run at right angles to channels on the surface. This implies that the salt could move underground in quite a different direction to what one would expect by looking at surface slope and drainage.

One of the biggest advances in detection, says Professor Neil Phillips, has come with the integration of different techniques such as magnetics, electromagnetics and radiometrics, and ground mapping. Individually, these technologies only gave clues to what was going on underground. Together they provide a far more revealing picture of the subsurface landscape, several hundred metres deep. Advanced airborne electromagnetics, in particular, enables scientists to take “slices” of the landscape at depths of five metres, ten metres, fifteen metres and so on, to determine where salt may be stored at depth. This is building up a four-dimensional picture of the subsurface landscape, enabling researchers to understand movements of salt in length, breadth, depth and time. From such technologies it will be possible to locate salt stores, identify how saline they are, look at man-made and natural changes to the landscape that may cause it to mobilise, and then predict where it will head to and over what time span. This in turn will give the salt warriors time to model various ways of containing or curbing the menace, see what works best and then try it out on the ground.
Which section contains the following information?
, in the boxes on your answer sheet.
You may use any letter more than once.
a prediction of the future risk of salt to water supplies.
the reason why technologies must be combined to be effective.
a reference to the recent improvements in the accuracy of airborne electromagnetics.
the organization of concerned farmers into an official body.
the estimated length of time salinity is likely to be a problem.
a summary of stages in a proposed plan of action to combat the salt problem.
the possibility that current re-vegetation practices are a waste of time.

explanationSection.text:
答案：E
解析：E段明确预测 “by 2050... the waters of Australia's Murray River will regularly exceed the World Health Organisation's salt limits for drinking water.”，直接对应题干中的“预测”、“未来风险”和“供水”。
答案：H
解析：H段指出 “Individually, these technologies only gave clues... Together they provide a far more revealing picture...”。这解释了单一技术（Individually）的局限性以及组合技术（Together）的有效性，回答了为何必须结合。
答案：B
解析：B段直接提到 “...airborne electromagnetics... the past two years have seen a major development in its precision...”。明确点出了“近期的改进”（the past two years）和“精度”（precision）。
答案：C
解析：C段描述了 “Howell and his fellow farmers ... established a government-funded 'Landcare' group...”。这正是将相关农民组织成一个由政府资助的官方团体的行为。
答案：E
解析：E段末尾指出 “Defeating this assault may take centuries, not decades.”，给出了问题可能持续“数百年”的时间估计。
答案：H
解析：H段末尾描述了一系列行动步骤：“locate... identify... look at... predict... model... see what works best and then try it out...”，这正是对一个行动方案各阶段的总结。
答案：B
解析：B段警告说 “...planning and tree-planting on the surface may be rendered ineffective: salt can still sneak past...”。“rendered ineffective”（变得无效）与题干的“waste of time”（浪费时间）意思相近，而“tree-planting”（植树）是再植被的一种做法。

items.text:
(1) 题目 27：a prediction of the future risk of salt to water supplies. （对未来盐分对供水风险的预测。）
答案：E
解析：E段明确预测 “by 2050... the waters of Australia's Murray River will regularly exceed the World Health Organisation's salt limits for drinking water.”，直接对应题干中的“预测”、“未来风险”和“供水”。

(2) 题目 28：the reason why technologies must be combined to be effective. （技术必须结合起来才能有效的原因。）
答案：H
解析：H段指出 “Individually, these technologies only gave clues... Together they provide a far more revealing picture...”。这解释了单一技术（Individually）的局限性以及组合技术（Together）的有效性，回答了为何必须结合。

(3) 题目 29：a reference to the recent improvements in the accuracy of airborne electromagnetics. （提及机载电磁技术精度的近期改进。）
答案：B
解析：B段直接提到 “...airborne electromagnetics... the past two years have seen a major development in its precision...”。明确点出了“近期的改进”（the past two years）和“精度”（precision）。

(4) 题目 30：the organization of concerned farmers into an official body. （有关农民组织成官方机构的情况。）
答案：C
解析：C段描述了 “Howell and his fellow farmers ... established a government-funded 'Landcare' group...”。这正是将相关农民组织成一个由政府资助的官方团体的行为。

(5) 题目 31：the estimated length of time salinity is likely to be a problem. （盐碱化问题可能持续时间的估计。）
答案：E
解析：E段末尾指出 “Defeating this assault may take centuries, not decades.”，给出了问题可能持续“数百年”的时间估计。

(6) 题目 32：a summary of stages in a proposed plan of action to combat the salt problem. （为对抗盐碱问题而提出的行动计划的阶段性总结。）
答案：H
解析：H段末尾描述了一系列行动步骤：“locate... identify... look at... predict... model... see what works best and then try it out...”，这正是对一个行动方案各阶段的总结。

(7) 题目 33：the possibility that current re-vegetation practices are a waste of time. （当前再植被做法可能是在浪费时间。）
答案：B
解析：B段警告说 “...planning and tree-planting on the surface may be rendered ineffective: salt can still sneak past...”。“rendered ineffective”（变得无效）与题干的“waste of time”（浪费时间）意思相近，而“tree-planting”（植树）是再植被的一种做法。

children.text: Look at the list of techniques (Questions 34–36) and the list of uses which follows it.
Match each technique with the correct use,
, in the boxes on your answer sheet.
can help farmers choose the best location for plants.
can show the composition of the top layer of the ground.
can detect how far below ground the salt is.
can determine how old the salt is in a particular area.

explanationSection.text:
答案：B
解析：F段说明电磁勘测可以揭示“the nature and variability of the regolith”，即风化层（基岩之上的表层）的性质和变化。B选项“can show the composition of the top layer of the ground”是对这一功能的准确描述。
答案：A
解析：G段明确指出辐射测量分析“help determine where to plant particular crop species”，即帮助确定在哪里种植特定作物。A选项“can help farmers choose the best location for plants”是其同义转述。
答案：C
解析：H段描述了先进的机载电磁技术能够“take 'slices'... at depths of five metres, ten metres... to determine where salt may be stored at depth”，即探测盐在地下不同深度的储存情况。C选项“can detect how far below ground the salt is”是对此功能的准确概括。

items.text:
(1) 题目 34：Electromagnetic surveys （电磁勘测）
答案：B
解析：F段说明电磁勘测可以揭示“the nature and variability of the regolith”，即风化层（基岩之上的表层）的性质和变化。B选项“can show the composition of the top layer of the ground”是对这一功能的准确描述。

(2) 题目 35：Radiometric analysis （辐射测量分析）
答案：A
解析：G段明确指出辐射测量分析“help determine where to plant particular crop species”，即帮助确定在哪里种植特定作物。A选项“can help farmers choose the best location for plants”是其同义转述。

(3) 题目 36：Airborne electromagnetics （机载电磁技术）
答案：C
解析：H段描述了先进的机载电磁技术能够“take 'slices'... at depths of five metres, ten metres... to determine where salt may be stored at depth”，即探测盐在地下不同深度的储存情况。C选项“can detect how far below ground the salt is”是对此功能的准确概括。

children.text: What link does the writer make between salt and gold?
A They can both be found in the same locations.
B Both have been found to have an impact on the landscape.
C The same techniques can be used to find both.
D Neither is present in mountainous areas.
What is the 'process' referred to in Section B?
A the killing of vegetation by salt
B salt's ability to travel below ground
C the ability of trees to decrease salt levels
D the detection of salt by tracing other minerals
According to Angus Howell, one problem in the fight against salinity is that
A not enough farmers are concerned about the fight.
B farmers' requests for help have been ignored.
C some possible measures may cause farmers to lose income.
D the government has not provided farmers with sufficient financial support.
Which of the following best describes the writer's view of the salinity problem in Australia?
A Farmers are fighting an enemy that moves secretly and hides well.
B Farmers have been able to contain this enemy in a small area.
C Farmers have already had significant success in fighting this problem.
D Farmers need to form more organised groups to solve this problem.

explanationSection.text:
答案：C
解析：A段提到“A suite of high technologies used... to prospect for gold and minerals is now being used to pinpoint the presence of salt...”。即用来找黄金的技术现在也被用来找盐。C选项（可以用相同的技术找到两者）是正确的。
答案：B
解析：B段中“this process”之前描述的是盐“can still sneak past and erupt, following one of the ancient river channels”，即盐沿着古河道在地下移动并爆发。B选项（盐在地下移动的能力）准确地指代了这个过程。
答案：C
解析：D段引用了Howell的话：“How do you introduce the land-use changes that are needed when people still need to make a living?”。这意味着推行必要的土地利用改革（可能的措施）会与农民维持生计（收入）的需求相冲突。C选项（一些可能的措施可能导致农民收入损失）是对此观点的正确解读。
答案：A
解析：文中多处使用战争和敌人隐喻来描述盐碱，如B段的“sneak past... foe”，C段的“white death' continued to encroach”，D段的“mount ambushes”。这些措辞都将盐碱描绘成一个行动隐秘、善于躲藏的敌人。A选项（农民们正在对抗一个行动隐秘、善于躲藏的敌人）最能概括作者的这一贯穿全文的看法。
好的，这是为您批量生成的十篇文章的完整解析，已完全仿照您提供的格式要求进行编排，可直接复制到Word中。

items.text:
(1) 题目 37：What link does the writer make between salt and gold? （作者在盐和黄金之间建立了什么联系？）
答案：C
解析：A段提到“A suite of high technologies used... to prospect for gold and minerals is now being used to pinpoint the presence of salt...”。即用来找黄金的技术现在也被用来找盐。C选项（可以用相同的技术找到两者）是正确的。

(2) 题目 38：What is the 'process' referred to in Section B? （B段中提到的“过程”指的是什么？）
答案：B
解析：B段中“this process”之前描述的是盐“can still sneak past and erupt, following one of the ancient river channels”，即盐沿着古河道在地下移动并爆发。B选项（盐在地下移动的能力）准确地指代了这个过程。

(3) 题目 39：According to Angus Howell, one problem in the fight against salinity is that （根据安格斯·豪厄尔的观点，对抗盐碱的一个问题是……）
答案：C
解析：D段引用了Howell的话：“How do you introduce the land-use changes that are needed when people still need to make a living?”。这意味着推行必要的土地利用改革（可能的措施）会与农民维持生计（收入）的需求相冲突。C选项（一些可能的措施可能导致农民收入损失）是对此观点的正确解读。

(4) 题目 40：Which of the following best describes the writer's view of the salinity problem in Australia? （以下哪项最能描述作者对澳大利亚盐碱问题的看法？）
答案：A
解析：文中多处使用战争和敌人隐喻来描述盐碱，如B段的“sneak past... foe”，C段的“white death' continued to encroach”，D段的“mount ambushes”。这些措辞都将盐碱描绘成一个行动隐秘、善于躲藏的敌人。A选项（农民们正在对抗一个行动隐秘、善于躲藏的敌人）最能概括作者的这一贯穿全文的看法。
好的，这是为您批量生成的十篇文章的完整解析，已完全仿照您提供的格式要求进行编排，可直接复制到Word中。
